explanation: This property formalizes the requirement that for every DM_i, a route calculation message (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) must only be sent after all of the following queries have occurred: to the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set components. That is, the sending of the route calculation message is allowed if and only if these four queries were all satisfied before. The selected template expresses that globally, if a route calculation message is sent, then it is preceded (implied by the 'until'-like G operator) by the querying of all the required sets.

The property states that, for all time, if a routeCalculation message is sent from the Local Node to DM_i (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi), then it is always the case beforehand that the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This is encoded as: G (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> G[0,] !( !(local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) ) ) . That is, whenever the routeCalculation message is sent, it is only possible if all four queries have already happened. This template applies to every DM_i since the property holds universally per index.

The requirement is that the routeCalculation message must be sent only after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set, and this must hold for every DM_i instantiation. To capture 'only after' for all instances, we universally require that whenever a routeCalculation message is sent, it is always preceded by queries to each of the four components. This is modeled using the template: 'G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] !bool_exp3)', where 'bool_exp1' expresses that a routeCalculation message is sent, and 'bool_exp3' expresses that any of the four required queries hasn't happened yet. Therefore, we state that if a routeCalculation message is sent, it must never be the case that any of the required queries have not already occurred. Alternatively, this prohibits the sending of a routeCalculation message until all necessary queries have happened. The 'G' captures the 'every DM_i' part (if indexed by i in the APs).

The property requires that for every DM_i, a routeCalculation message (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) may occur only after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This can be formally described by mandating that globally, whenever a routeCalculation message is sent, it implies that the Local Node has previously queried all the required sets. This temporal relation is captured by universally requiring that the routeCalculation message is never sent before the conjunction of all component queries (Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, Multiple Interface Association) has held at least once. By expressing that the routeCalculation message cannot be sent unless all conditions are met, we ensure compliance for every DM_i.

This property states that, for every DM_i, the routeCalculation message is sent only after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association components. The formula encodes that, at all times, if a routeCalculation message is sent, then it must be the case that the four queried events have already occurred (possibly in any order, but before the routeCalculation is sent). This matches the intent of the original statement: sending the routeCalculation message must be preceded by the set of required queries for every DM_i.

The input states that a route-calculation message may only be sent after the Local Node has queried (i.e., checked/interacted with) all four components: Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This 'only after' sequencing is a canonical precedence safety constraint, best represented in LTL by stating that at any point where the routeCalculation message is sent, all the query actions for the relevant components must have occurred before. That is, it is never allowed for a routeCalculation message to be sent at a point where any of the prerequisite queries is still pending. This can be encoded as: it is always the case that if routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi occurs, then it must be the case that all of local_node_queried_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_topology_information_base, and local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association have occurred beforehand. The simplest LTL template to encode this is to state that at all times, if the route calculation message is sent, then at that moment, it is not the case that any of the prerequisites have not occurred yet (i.e., it's never the case that routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi happens while not all previous queries have been satisfied). This is best mapped to index 0: G[0,] !bool_exp3, with bool_exp3 being 'routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi & (!(local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association))'. This states: "It is always not the case that a route calculation message is sent before all the required queries have taken place." This correctly models the 'only after' semantics requested in the property, and with 'for every DM_i' universalized by the atomic proposition naming convention.

The property requires that the route-calculation message (routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi) can only be sent after the Local Node has queried all four components: Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This must hold for every DM_i, which is reflected by a global (G) restriction. This is a classic 'only after all previous events' property, modeled as: at any time, if the routecalc_msg is sent, then it must have been preceded by all four queries. In LTL, this is typically expressed by forbidding routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi until all four queries have happened. Template 80, '!bool_exp3 U bool_exp4', is suited for expressing 'an event (here: route calculation message sent) is not allowed until a set of conditions are true (all required queries performed)'. Thus, routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi is forbidden until (local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_topology_information_base & local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association) is true.

The property states that for every DM_i, the route-calculation message must only be sent after the Local Node has queried the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set. This is captured by requiring that the event "routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi" does not occur until all of "local_node_queried_neighbor_set", "local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set", "local_node_queried_topology_information_base", and "local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association" have occurred. This is well-captured by using the 'until' operator: it globally forbids sending the route calculation message until all the required queries have occurred in the past (or at that instant). The 'and' between the four queries expresses that all must have happened prior to allowing the route calculation message to be sent. This is a safety property valid at all times.

The property requires that, for every DM_i, the route-calculation message is only sent after the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set have all been queried by the Local Node. This can be enforced in LTL by saying that, globally, whenever the routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi is true, it must be preceded (in any run) by local_node_queried_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_two_hop_neighbor_set, local_node_queried_topology_information_base, and local_node_queried_multiple_interface_association all being true at some point earlier. The best LTL template to capture this causal/pre-condition behavior is one expressing an 'only after' (causal-precedence) relationship. Template 93 is suitable: G (bool_exp1 -> (bool_exp3 U[0,] bool_exp4)). Here, bool_exp1 asserts the property for every DM_i (which in LTL is captured globally, but the phrasing is already for every DM_i), bool_exp3 is the requirement that all four queries have happened, and bool_exp4 is the route-calculation message being sent. The 'U[0,]' encapsulates the 'only after' or 'until' nature (the queries must all happen before the route calculation message can be sent).

This LTL property expresses that, for every DM_i (modeled as universally quantified by the template structure), whenever a routeCalculation message is sent by the Local Node to DM_i (represented by 'routecalc_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi'), it must be the case that before this action, the Neighbor Set, 2 Hop Neighbor Set, Topology Information Base, and Multiple Interface Association Set have all been queried by the Local Node. This is captured by the temporal logic formula, which ensures that route calculation cannot happen unless all the required queries have occurred first. The chosen template encodes this requirement as a safety property for all computations.
